"name": "Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)"}], "description": "A flaw was found in Keycloak. An authenticated attacker can perform Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) by manipulating the `client_session_host` parameter during refresh token requests. This occurs when a Keycloak client is configured to use the `backchannel.logout.url` with the `application.session.host` placeholder. Successful exploitation allows the attacker to make HTTP requests from the Keycloak server’s network context, potentially probing internal networks or internal APIs, leading to information disclosure.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00295, "percentile": 0.21512}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-22rm-wp4x-v5cx", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-03-27T20:03:17Z", "html_url":
